Bikaji Foods – Systematix Detailed View

Growth Drivers
• Aggressive distribution expansion (~50,000 outlets added annually)
• Diversification into retail/QSR, frozen foods and bakery segments
• Superior pack/SKU mix supporting better margins
• Higher A&P spend strengthening brand positioning
• Strong pricing power with minimal impact on volumes
• Ability to maintain margins despite input cost pressures
• Entering low-capex, high free cash flow phase
• Improving capacity utilisation over next 2–3 years

Key Risks
• Intense competition may impact growth
• Raw material inflation could pressure margins
• Region-specific disruptions may affect sales

Overall Impact
• Positive driven by strong growth visibility and improving cash flows

Nazara Technologies – Acquisition Commentary

Deal Overview
• Acquiring Buletile & Bestplay, both strong cash-generating businesses
• Total payout (including earn-outs) estimated at ~$350 million by June 2030

Structure & Funding
• $100 million for initial 50% stake
• Additional EBITDA-linked payments by 2028
• Performance-based earn-outs
• May require debt or additional funding over time
• Potential fund raise of ₹1,000–2,000 Cr depending on expansion

Growth Impact
• Acquisition expected to at least double revenue by next year

Overall Impact
• Positive driven by scale-up and strong cash-generating assets

Saatvik Green / Saatvik Energy – Management Commentary

Strategy & Vision
• Aiming to become a fully integrated player in solar manufacturing ecosystem
• Expansion aligned with ALMM-III timelines
• Supports India’s long-term energy independence vision

Growth & Industry Tailwinds
• Government push for domestic manufacturing to aid growth
• Bottomline improvement expected over the next few years

Cost & Supply Chain Dynamics
• Ingot-wafer costs currently higher vs imports (especially China)
• Cost competitiveness expected to improve as ecosystem matures
• Domestic supply chain for ingots to strengthen gradually
• Import dependence likely to reduce over time

Overall Impact
• Positive in the long term driven by integration and policy support

**HDFC Bank – Emkay View**

**Key Observations**
• No corporate governance concerns post management interaction
• Some operational issues still persist and remain unresolved
• Differences at management level causing internal disruptions
• Stabilisation expected but may take time

**Rating & Preference**
• BUY rating maintained with no change in estimates
• Lower preference vs ICICI Bank, Axis Bank and Kotak Mahindra Bank

**Overall Impact**
• Neutral to slightly positive

EMKAY ON HDFC BANK

Post the conference call with the management we are completely assured that there are no corporate governance issues in HDFC Bank

We are not completely assured that there are no operational issues in the bank

There seems to be some differences of opinion at the management level leading to these ruffles

We see that it will take some time for the bank to settle all such internal issues

We have not changed any of our estimates so we continue to retain our BUY rating on HDFC Bank

At approximately 85% plant utilization, the facility has the potential to generate annual revenues exceeding ₹1,000 crore, which is 5x of company's current revenue run-rate

China’s demand for gold remains robust:

China officially bought 1 tonne of gold in February, marking the 16th consecutive monthly purchase.

Over this period, China has acquired 50 tonnes of gold. 

By comparison, in 2022-2024, the buying streak lasted for 18 straight months. 

As a result, China’s total gold reserves are up to a record 2,309 tonnes, or 10% of foreign exchange reserves.

The percentage has DOUBLED over the last 20 months.

At the current gold price of ~$5,000/oz, China now owns ~$371 billion of gold.

China is holding more gold than ever.

text:
Indian Economy & Markets – Key Updates (18 March 2026)

Macro & Policy Developments
• India’s chemicals industry projected to reach $255 billion by 2030 (McKinsey)
• ₹11,440 Cr Pulses Mission approved to drive self-sufficiency by 2030-31
• Government pushes coconut sector to strengthen global leadership
• ₹1,500 Cr solar recycling initiative launched to support circular economy

Corporate & Sectoral Tailwinds
• GCC expansion and flex spaces expected to boost office demand in 2026
• Honda to invest ₹1,200 Cr in EV SUV (‘O Alpha’) manufacturing in Rajasthan

Global Cues & Commodities
• Brent crude rebounds above $105 amid Iran conflict escalation
• Rising geopolitical tensions revive supply risks in energy markets

Equity Markets Snapshot
• Sensex hits 1-week high at 76,704, extending 3-day rally
• IT stocks lead gains: Tech Mahindra, Infosys, HCLTech, TCS
• Laggards include NTPC, HUL, Sun Pharma

Mutual Fund & Wealth Creation Insights
• ₹10,000 SIP grows to ₹1.97 Cr in Canara Robeco Large & Mid Cap Fund (~21 years, 16.89% XIRR)
• Edelweiss MF wins Asset Manager of the Year (second time), highlighting disciplined investing
• Parag Parikh Flexi Cap Fund among top Morningstar 2026 winners

Commodities & Investment Trends
• Gold and Silver ETFs decline up to 4% ahead of Fed decision
• Investors turn cautious amid rate uncertainty and global tensions

**Nuvama on Cement Sector – Key Takeaways**

**Overall View**
• Remains positive on the cement sector
• JK Cement highlighted as the top pick

**Demand Trends**
• Cement demand stayed healthy in Feb-26
• Expected to remain strong in Q4
• Temporary softness likely in March due to labour disruptions

**Government Spending**
• Capex trend mixed with a short-term dip in Jan-26
• Overall FY26 government spending still higher on a YoY basis

**Housing Segment**
• Weak housing supply
• Sharp decline in new project launches

**Pricing Scenario**
• Prices largely stable in Mar-26 after earlier hikes and rollbacks
• Dealers expect fresh price hikes in late March to early April

**Cost Pressures**
• Rising pet coke and packaging costs
• Likely to drive price increases
• Key monitorable for margins going forward

**Takeaway**
• Strong demand outlook with pricing support expected
• Margins dependent on ability to pass on rising input costs

DEE Development Engineers : Co. Starts Seamless Pipe Plant Production

• 🏭 Commercial Production Begins: Seamless pipe manufacturing plant commissioned

• 📍 New Facility: Located in Kutch, Gujarat

• ⚙️ Capacity Added: ~7,000 MT per annum of seamless pipes & tubes

• 💰 Capex Investment: ~₹ 89.74 Cr (above materiality threshold)

**KHAZANCHI JEWELLERS: B2B → B2C SHIFT DRIVING A STRUCTURAL MARGIN UPGRADE**

Most investors focus on revenue growth.
Smart money tracks where the margin pool is shifting. 📊

**Khazanchi is moving from a wholesale-led B2B model to a retail-led B2C model — and the numbers explain why this matters.**

In B2B, margins are capped at ~4.5–5% despite scale.
In B2C, margins are already at ~9–10% and expected to expand to 12–13% with premium and branded jewellery mix. 💎

**That’s a 2–3x margin expansion opportunity without changing the core product**

The transition is visible:
B2C contribution is expected to rise from single-digit levels to ~20–25% by FY27, shifting the earnings engine 📈

Execution is backing the story:
A 10,000 sq ft flagship showroom with ~₹550 Cr revenue potential acts as a key catalyst for retail scale 🏬

**Now the real insight:**

Higher B2C mix → Higher margins
Higher margins → Better return ratios
Better return ratios → Valuation re-rating

Same gold.
But a completely different business model

text:
Polymer Price Surge Triggers Shutdowns in Plastic Industry

Polymer prices have surged up to ~70% due to supply disruptions from West Asia conflict

Sharp rise in key inputs like HDPE, LDPE, LLDPE, PVC, and PP

Example: HDPE prices jumped from ~₹91,000 to ~₹1.47 lakh/tonne (~60%+)

Many plastic-processing units are unable to pass on higher costs due to fixed contracts

Result: widespread shutdowns, especially in Odisha where 50–75% units have halted production

Industry faces raw material shortages and pricing uncertainty

Sector has ~50,000 enterprises, with ~85% MSMEs, making it highly vulnerable

Provides employment to ~5 million people, raising concerns over job impact

Smaller players are the most affected due to weak pricing power

Highlights dependence on imported petrochemical supply chains

2. Forecasts for rates little changed

4. Number looking for multiple cuts now 5 vs 8 earlier

8. If Kevin Warsh is not confirmed by the June meeting, Powell would stay on as chair pro tem
9. Moreover, he will stay on as governor as long as the investigation into Powell’s behavior is ongoing
10.Powell indicated that rate policy implications of higher productivity aren't necessarily dovish

So when the time came to choose between Adani and Vedanta, it wasn't really a "committee" deciding

It was effectively one government-backed entity with 86% of the vote

Yesterday, Adani bought Jaypee Group for Rs 14,535 Cr 

But Vedanta had bid around Rs 17,000Cr, over Rs 2,000 Cr MORE than Adani

Yet their higher bid lost and Adani won the bid despite a lower bid!

So how did the lowest bid win one of India's largest bankruptcy deals?

The lenders reasoned that this was because of a higher upfront payment and faster timeline

Vedanta called this unfair and challenged it at NCLT, but got rejected yesterday

But here's the thing, this wasn't really the "lenders" deciding 

It was actually NARCL

Telephone Evolution: From 1667 String to 2024 AI Smartphones

Key Milestones  
- 1667 UK: Hooke’s string phone (acoustic)  
- 1844 US: Morse telegraph  
- 1876 US: Bell’s first telephone (liquid transmitter)  
- 1878 US: Edison’s carbon microphone

Tech Shift  
From acoustic wires to AI-powered foldables, telephony drove connectivity revolution. Next: AR/VR integration?

🚨 PVR CINEMAS REVENUE MODEL.

If you pay ₹200 for a ticket, how much does the theatre keep?

About 50%. The other 50% goes to the movie producer.

The split changes based on performance.
If the film doesn’t hit the required numbers, PVR gets a 2.5% bonus.

So the split becomes:
PVR: 52.5%
Producer: 47.5%

If the film performs well, the producer earns more in later weeks.

The theatre’s share can drop to:

37.5% in the next week
and then ~30% later.

So the bigger the film runs,
the more money shifts to the producer per ticket.

➤ Samref Refinery — Saudi Arabia
➤ Al Hosn Gas Field — UAE
➤ Jubail Petrochemical Complex — Saudi Arabia
➤ Mesaieed Petrochemical Complex — Qatar
➤ Ras Laffan Refinery — Qatar

The report added: “Previously, clear and repeated warnings were given to your rulers about entering this dangerous path and gambling with the fate of their nations.”

Apollo Hospitals: 4,444 Beds Planned, Only 3,630 Census Beds

Apollo targets 4,444 bed addition, but investors focus on 3,630 census beds for revenue impact.

Census Beds Explained  
- Overnight stay beds: General/special wards, ICU  
- Drive ARPOB, occupancy rates, ALOS metrics  
- Key for hospital profitability analysis  

Non-Census Beds  
- No overnight stays: Emergency, daycare, dialysis, surgical  
- Support ops but excluded from core capacity metrics  
- Total beds = census + non-census (incomplete picture alone)  

Investor Lesson  
Differentiate census vs non-census additions before valuing capacity growth. Examples: Apollo, Max, KIMS. Focus census for true revenue potential.

Market Snapshot  
- MCap: ₹16,017.83 Cr  
- Belrise Industries (formerly Badve Engineering) acquires Chester Hall Precision Engineering Holdings Ltd, UK, marking entry into global aerospace and defense manufacturing.  

Deal Highlights  
- Enterprise Value: £13.2 million  
- Chester Hall Revenue: ~£18.5 million  
- Valuation at 6x EV/EBITDA, expected to be EPS- and ROCE-accretive from day one.  

Strategic Impact  
- Provides Belrise entry into aerospace, space, and defense precision engineering markets.  
- Chester Hall is known for machining aerostructures, aero-engine components, and satellite parts for leading OEMs.  
- Acquisition adds advanced certifications and strong OEM linkages to Belrise’s capabilities.  

Synergy Advantage  
- Combines Belrise’s manufacturing scale in India and France with Chester Hall’s precision engineering expertise.  
- Positions Belrise as an emerging global Tier-1 partner in aerospace manufacturing and strengthens India’s role in the international defense supply chain.  

Key Takeaway:  
The UK acquisition marks Belrise’s strategic diversification beyond automotive — establishing a high-value foothold in global aerospace and defense precision engineering.

Polymer Prices Surge 70%, Triggering Industry Shutdowns  

Price Shock  
- Key polymers up ~70% due to West Asia conflict supply disruptions.  
- HDPE: ₹91,000 → ₹1.47 lakh/tonne (~60% jump).  
- Affected: HDPE, LDPE, LLDPE, PVC, PP.  

Operational Impact  
- Units unable to pass costs due to fixed contracts.  
- 50–75% shutdowns in Odisha; widespread elsewhere.  
- Raw material shortages and pricing uncertainty intensify crisis.  

Industry Profile  
- ~50,000 enterprises, 85% MSMEs — highly vulnerable.  
- Employs ~5 million people, raising job loss concerns.  

Structural Vulnerability  
- Smaller players hit hardest due to weak pricing power.  
- Exposes imported petrochemical dependence.  

Key Takeaway:  
70% polymer surge forces mass shutdowns in plastics sector, threatening 5M jobs and highlighting urgent need for supply chain resilience.

Decade-Long Transformation

- Per capita consumption: 957 kWh → 1,460 kWh.  

Infrastructure Leap  
- Transmission expansion sharply cut AT&C losses.  
- Peak demand: ~250 GW, signaling robust economic activity.  
- Saubhagya & DDUGJY schemes drove near-universal electrification.  

Clean Energy Push  
- Renewables scaled rapidly (solar, wind, green hydrogen).  
- One Nation, One Grid enhances integration/reliability.  
- Energy storage (battery + pumped) and nuclear targeting ~100 GW by 2047.  

Efficiency Reforms  
- RDSS digital reforms boost accountability.  

Demand Drivers  
- Surging needs from AI, data centres, industry.  
- India emerging as clean energy/manufacturing hub with cross-border trade focus.  

Key Takeaway:  
India’s power sector transformation — from shortages to surplus renewables — fuels economic growth and global clean energy leadership.

Audi India Forecasts 5–7% Luxury Car Growth in 2026  

Market Outlook  
- Luxury car market expected to grow ~5–7% in 2026 — moderate vs prior double-digit hopes.  
- India’s luxury segment still small at ~50,000 units annually.  

Growth Headwinds  
- Entry-level luxury weakening due to rising prices and affordability challenges.  
- Stable but not high-growth environment ahead.  

Product Strategy  
- Recently launched Audi SQ8 performance SUV priced at ~₹1.78 crore.  
- Focus on “right-priced, right-sized” models to boost EV adoption.  

Positioning  
- Premium end demand resilient, but entry-level affordability remains constrained.  
- Strengthening electrification push in luxury segment.  

Key Takeaway:  
Luxury auto growth moderates to 5–7% amid entry-level pressures, but Audi’s EV-focused strategy and premium resilience offer steady upside.

India’s RE Boom Hits Grid Bottlenecks  

Capacity vs Infrastructure Gap  
- ~200 GW solar + wind capacity installed (Feb 2026).  
- Grid expansion lagging, risking 35 GW curtailment by FY27.  

Curtailment Crisis  
- 39% average curtailment recently, some states hitting 50%.  
- TGNA projects (temporary grid access) face 80% of curtailments vs secure LTGNA.  
- Worst affected: Rajasthan & Gujarat (~45% of RE capacity).  

Root Causes  
- Daytime solar surplus due to generation-demand mismatch.  
- Impacts project economics: IRR down ~150 bps, DSCR drops ~10%.  

Near-Term Cushions  
- Supported by loan moratoriums and reserve buffers.  
- Many TGNA projects to convert to long-term access in 10–12 months.  

Emerging Solutions  
- Transmission expansion, grid access reforms, policy support.  
- Battery storage and split scheduling for better utilisation.  

Key Takeaway:  
Grid constraints threaten 35 GW RE capacity, but transmission upgrades and storage solutions can unlock India’s renewable growth potential.

India’s Power Sector Emerges as New Growth Engine  

Transformation Journey  
- India’s electricity ecosystem has shifted from power shortages to surplus capacity over the past decade.  
- Total generation surged from ~1,020 BU (FY14) to ~1,830 BU (FY25).  
- Per capita consumption rose from 957 kWh to 1,460 kWh, driven by improved access and rising demand.  

Infrastructure & Efficiency Gains  
- Transmission expansion and schemes like Saubhagya and Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Gram Jyoti Yojana enabled near-universal electrification.  
- AT&C losses reduced sharply, while peak demand hit ~250 GW, reflecting robust economic momentum.  
- One Nation, One Grid, One Frequency ensures seamless integration and reliability.  

Renewable & Future Focus  
- Renewable capacity scaled rapidly with solar, wind, and green hydrogen initiatives.  
- Push for energy storage (batteries + pumped storage) and nuclear expansion targeting ~100 GW by 2047.  
- Digital reforms like Revamped Distribution Sector Scheme (RDSS) boost efficiency and accountability.  

Demand Drivers  
- Surging power needs from AI, data centres, and industrial growth.  
- India emerging as a global clean energy hub with cross-border trade and regional collaboration.  

Key Takeaway:  
India’s power sector is powering economic growth with surplus capacity, renewable push, and efficiency reforms, positioning the country as a clean energy leader.

• Strong export oriented refining model
• Advantage in complex refining and product mix

• Oil power is shifting from resource ownership to processing capability
• Refining scale plus complexity defines global energy influence

**Key Takeaway:** Nomura bullish on M&M's multi-segment momentum and EV profitability inflection.

@Stockupdate9

**CLSA on IT Services: No AI Pricing Pressure, Valuations Attractive**  

**Renewal Dynamics & Decision Delays**  
- **No AI-led pricing pressure** observed in contract renewals.  
- Client decisions delayed by **AI adoption and West Asia crisis**.  
- **Direct West Asia exposure low**; macro impact remains uncertain.  

**Deal Pipeline & Valuation Opportunity**  
- **Strong deal pipelines** across the sector.  
- Valuations at **10-year average levels**, presenting attractive entry points.  

**Stock Recommendations**  
- **High Conviction:** Persistent, Coforge.  
- **Outperform:** Infosys, Tech Mahindra, TCS, LTIM.  
- **Hold:** HCL Tech, Wipro.  

**Key Takeaway:** CLSA sees IT services resilient amid headwinds, with compelling valuations and robust deal momentum.

@Stockupdate9

**Morgan Stanley on Reliance Industries: Maintains OVERWEIGHT, Target ₹1,803 (unchanged)**  

**Major Green Ammonia Deal**  
- Signs **$3 bn deal with Samsung C&T** for green ammonia supply.  
- Agreement spans **15 years**, marking key monetisation of new energy investments.  

**Strategic & Valuation Highlights**  
- **Aligned with hydrogen and decarbonisation strategy**.  
- New energy business valued at **~$20 bn**; energy earnings set to rise further.  
- Stock trades at **~62% discount to peers**.  

**Key Takeaway:** Morgan Stanley sees Reliance's green energy push gaining strong commercial traction with this landmark deal.

@Stockupdate9

**Goldman Sachs on BEL: Maintains BUY**  

**Strong Order Inflow Momentum**  
- Announces **₹10.1 bn order inflow**.  
- **FY26 order book reaches ₹216.2 bn** so far.  

**Diverse Order Portfolio**  
- Covers **communication and radar systems**.  
- Includes **electro optic systems and fire control**.  
- Encompasses **high energy laser and software solutions**.  

**Key Takeaway:** Goldman Sachs highlights BEL's robust order pipeline across defence electronics, reinforcing growth visibility.

@Stockupdate9

**Nomura on Indian Hotels (CMP: ₹622.05): Maintains BUY, Target Cut to ₹800**  

**Limited Q4 War Impact**  
- **War impact on Q4FY26 expected limited**.  
- **Q4FY26 revenue growth estimated at 11% YoY**, EBITDA at **12% YoY**.  

**Strong Growth Trajectory**  
- **EBITDA CAGR of 13–14%** over FY26–28 looks reasonable.  
- **Positive outlook for luxury segment** and corporate travel in medium term.  

**Key Takeaway:** Nomura sees Indian Hotels resilient amid disruptions, with healthy growth ahead in premium segments.

@Stockupdate9

**HDFC Sec on ASK Automotive: Initiates ADD, Target ₹480**  

**Content Growth Drivers**  
- Valued at **22x March 2028 EPS**.  
- **Premiumization and electrification** boosting content per vehicle.  
- **EVs carry 30–40% higher content** vs. ICE vehicles.  

**Margin Expansion Catalysts**  
- **Export mix growth** to support margin gains.  
- **Wheel assembly exit** to boost margins beyond FY27.  

**Near-Term Challenges**  
- **Higher raw material costs** creating headwinds.  
- **Geopolitical tensions** may pressure export demand.  

**Key Takeaway:** HDFC Sec sees strong long-term content growth for ASK Automotive despite near-term cost pressures.

@Stockupdate9

**JPMorgan on Oil & Gas: Volatility Persists Amid Geopolitical Risks**  

**Short-Term Earnings Upheaval**  
- **Crude volatility and commodity shortages** causing sharp earnings swings.  
- **Directional bets challenging**, especially at scale, given uncertainty.  

**Oil Price & Stock Outlook**  
- **FY27 oil prices likely higher** than mid-February forecasts across most scenarios.  
- **OMC stocks poised to rebound** on ceasefire announcements.  
- **Higher oil to boost ONGC, GAIL, Reliance** via positive medium-term revisions.  

**Relative Performance**  
- **GAIL underperformed NIFTY** since war began; **ONGC and Reliance outperformed** market.  

**Key Takeaway:** JPMorgan sees elevated oil prices supporting upstream majors despite near-term volatility.

@Stockupdate9

**Elara Capital on PVR Inox: Maintains BUY, Target ₹1,300 (unchanged)**  

**Dhurandhar 2 Box Office Blockbuster**  
- **Day one collections expected at ~₹1 bn**.  
- **Lifetime box office projected at ₹11–13 bn**.  
- **Strong franchise recall** driving higher footfalls; sequels typically outperform originals by **40–50%**.  

**Operational & Financial Recovery**  
- **Q4 occupancy at ~28%**; FY26 occupancy estimated at **26.5%**.  
- **Higher ATP** from premium formats boosting revenues.  
- **Net debt to drop sharply** from ₹3.6 bn to ₹1.4 bn.  
- **ROIC to improve to 10–12%** ahead.  

**Key Takeaway:** Elara sees Dhurandhar 2 as a major catalyst for PVR Inox's occupancy and profitability turnaround.

- **India's heavy reliance on Middle East feedstocks** causing widespread supply chain disruptions.  
- **Fertilizers hit hardest**, followed by paints, adhesives, and related segments.  
- **Crop protection firms** face risks from delayed kharif sowing timelines.  

**Margin & Inventory Outlook**  
- **Export margins protected** through cost pass-through mechanisms.  
- **Q4 sees inventory gains**, but **operating rate cuts and margin pressure** expected from April onwards.  

**Key Takeaway:** Jefferies flags near-term supply disruptions and margin risks for Indian chemicals, with Q4 inventory gains offering temporary relief.

**🇮🇳 INDIA SEMICONDUCTOR SECTOR – QUICK INVESTMENT SNAPSHOT**

**🧭 OVERALL VIEW**
• Early-stage capex-led upcycle
• Strong tailwinds from AI + electronics + policy
• Still import dependent + execution risk
• Strategy = selective play, not broad betting

**📊 DEMAND DRIVERS**
**Short Term (2–3 yrs)**
• Smartphones + electronics manufacturing boom
• 5G rollout + data centre expansion (AI infra push)
• China+1 shift (Apple, global OEMs)

**Long Term (5–10 yrs)**
• AI devices → higher chip content per device
• EVs + auto electronics + defence demand
• Cloud + AI ecosystem expansion

**🏭 SUPPLY SIDE POSITIONING**
• Strong in design (20% global talent)
• Emerging in ATMP/OSAT (Micron, Tata, CG Power JV)
• Weak in fabs & materials (import heavy)
• Focus = mature nodes (28–65nm), not advanced nodes

**🏗 KEY SEGMENT OPPORTUNITIES**
**Fabs / OSAT (Manufacturing)**
• High capex + high operating leverage
• Beneficiaries: Tata Electronics, Micron, CG Power JV
• Risk: execution + utilisation

**Fabless / IP (Best ROI Segment)**
• Asset-light, high margin
• Focus areas: edge-AI, EV chips, RF, analog
• Strong startup + design ecosystem

**Materials / Equipment (Import Substitution)**
• Huge opportunity (90% import dependency)
• Early stage → long runway
• Policy support via ECMS

**💰 MARGIN DRIVERS**
• Govt subsidy (up to 50% capex support)
• Operating leverage (utilisation ramp-up)
• Product mix (IP > manufacturing margins)
• Raw material import dependency (key risk)

**⚡️ KEY STRUCTURAL TRENDS**
• Shift to compute-driven AI economy
• Supply chain becoming security-driven (not cost-driven)
• India evolving → design → packaging → partial manufacturing
• Policy shift → ecosystem + IP creation focus

**🏛 POLICY SUPPORT**
• ₹76,000 Cr ISM + ₹40,000 Cr ECMS
• 50% subsidy for fabs
• Design incentives (DLI: 4–6% sales support)
• Tax benefits + talent push (85,000 VLSI engineers/year target)

**🌍 GLOBAL CONTEXT**
• Taiwan dominates advanced chips
• US leads design/IP
• China controls materials
• India = design + backend hub emerging

**📈 GROWTH OUTLOOK (2–3 YEARS)**
• Market growth: ~15–18% CAGR
• Early gains from ATMP/OSAT ramp-up
• Fabless design companies gaining traction
• Capex pipeline strong ($15B+ committed)

**⚠️ KEY RISKS**
• Execution delays (infra, fabs)
• High import dependency
• Low R&D spend (~0.6% GDP)
• Talent shortage / brain drain
• Future overcapacity risk

**🎯 INVESTMENT STRATEGY (MOST IMPORTANT)**
**Best Bet (High Conviction)**
• Fabless / IP companies (high margin, scalable)

**Second Layer**
• OSAT / ATMP players (operating leverage play)

**Early Stage Bets**
• Materials & equipment (import substitution theme)

**Avoid / Be Cautious**
• Low value assembly players
• Overhyped fab stories (long gestation, low ROCE risk)

**🧠 ONE LINE TAKE**
• India semiconductor = Design + Packaging story now, Manufacturing story later 🚀

**OIL SHOCK: NIFTY 15-20% RISK**  

**Thesis**  
- $25-30/bbl spike hits GDP 1-2pp, EPS 12%→5-7%, P/E 20x→17-18x.  
- Cyclical; digital/manuf/energy drives rebound.  

**Drivers**  
- GDP 6.5-6.7% to #3 by FY31.  
- Datacenters #2 APAC; EVs/H2/nuclear cut oil use.  
- Exports/domestic buffer.  

**Sectors**  
- **Severe neg**: OMCs (under-recoveries), aviation (ATF).  
- **Neg**: Financials (credit 7-8%), auto/chemicals/paints.  
- **Mild neg**: FMCG.  
- **Pos**: Upstream O&G, pharma, defence, digital infra.  

**Commentary**  
- $10/bbl = 0.2pp GDP drag; RBI delay 2-4Q.  

**Financials**  
- Crude bill $130-140 Bn; Nifty stress P/E 17-18x.  

**Valuations**  
- Normal 20-22x; post-bottom 90%+ gains.  

**Risks**  
- Crude persistence, rupee inflation, fiscal, China/Russia.  

**Triggers**  
- **Near**: Oil stabilise, rupee/RBI/yields.  
- **Med**: Credit revival, structural wins.  

**Bull/Bear**  
- **Bear**: 15-20% drop.  
- **Bull**: Quick peak →90% rally.  

**Key Takeaway:** Buy upstream/digital on weakness; await oil peak.

**KOTAK EPIC CHURN: DISTILLED EXTRACTION**  

**1. Investment Thesis**  
- Conflict correction = churn to quality, dump narratives/expensive.  
- Temporary crude hit; structural higher CoE leads 1-3Y derating.  

**2. Growth Drivers**  
- Nifty EPS CAGR: 8.2% FY26E, 16.2% FY27E, 14.8% FY28E (banks/oil/IT/metals).  
- GDP 7%/7.6%/6.8%; upstream/refiners gain short-term.  

**3. Segment Outlook**  
- **Financials**: Tier-2/3 banks/NBFCs cheap P/B.  
- **Consumption**: FMCG/QSR/retail/paints rich fwd P/E – reduce.  
- **Oil&Gas**: Upstream/refiners up; OMCs down.  
- **PSUs/Capgoods**: HAL/BHEL/IRFC/NHPC overvalued vs earnings/NW.  

**4. Broker View**  
- Base: Weeks conflict, months tension; SoH normalizes soon.  
- 1-2Q blips overpriced at 10-15% cuts.  

**5. Financials**  
- Nifty EPS: FY26E ₹1,072, FY27E ₹1,246, FY28E ₹1,432.  
- $10/bbl crude = $20 Bn CAD (0.5% GDP).  

**6. Valuations**  
- Nifty 19x FY27E, 16.5x FY28E.  
- BJFIN FV ₹2,500 (+41%); Eternal ₹375 (+70%).  

**7. Risks**  
- Prolonged SoH disruption/high crude/CAD strain.  
- Elections limit OMC pricing; faster derating.  

**8. Triggers**  
- **Near**: Conflict evolution, govt fuel stance, corp gas commentary.  
- **Medium**: Rates/disruption visibility, earnings delivery.  

**9. Bull/Bear**  
- **Bull**: Quick resolve, buy dips in quality (mid-teens Nifty growth).  
- **Bear**: Prolonged energy shock, broad derating lags earnings.  

**Key Takeaway:** Churn now: Financials in, consumption/PSUs out.

**Sahana System: Positioned for Growth in Anti-Drone Defense Amid Global Tensions**

Sahana System Limited is strategically expanding into the anti-drone (Counter-UAS) segment, aligning with rising global demand for advanced defense technologies.

With modern warfare increasingly driven by drone-based threats, governments are accelerating investments in detection, jamming and electronic warfare systems.

Sahana is capitalizing on this shift through RF and radar-based detection, AI-powered threat intelligence and integrated counter-drone solutions.

The company’s recent ₹24.69 crore order from Central Electronics Limited highlights strong execution and entry into defense electronics.

Partnership with Bharat Electronics Limited strengthens its position within India’s defense ecosystem.

Increasing demand from military, border security and critical infrastructure protection continues to support growth.

As geopolitical tensions persist, anti-drone technology is emerging as a critical defense priority.

Mankind Pharma Plans Early Semaglutide Launch in India

Mankind Pharma aims to be among the first companies to launch semaglutide in India after the drug’s patent expires on March 20.

India will be the primary launch market, while international markets will be explored later.

The company plans to launch the drug independently, with no co-marketing or partnership arrangements.

Semaglutide is widely used for diabetes and weight management, and demand is rising amid growing obesity and metabolic health concerns.

Mankind plans to price the drug carefully to balance affordability and long-term sustainability, continuing its strategy of introducing affordable medicines.

The company expects strong market expansion, as the patent expiry could allow around 50 generic brands to enter the Indian market.

Beyond diabetes, semaglutide has potential applications across cardiology, orthopaedics, and gynaecology, expanding its therapeutic scope.

**Marico Ltd — Sector Update | YES Securities | Mar-26**

**Analyst Call**
- Rating: **ADD** | Target Price: **₹850** (from ₹820)
- CMP: **₹752** | Upside: **~13%**

**Business Transformation**
- Shifting from legacy FMCG → **digital-first consumer powerhouse**
- New businesses to form **~33% of India revenues** by FY30
- Digital brands topline target: **₹40 Bn** by FY30
- Key digital brands: **Beardo (5x)**, **Plix (6x)**, **4700BC**, **Cosmix**

**Raw Material Tailwind**
- Copra down **14.8% QoQ** & **41% below recent peak**
- Easing copra + stable edible oil + packaging costs = **benign input environment**
- Management guiding **calibrated price corrections** to support volume recovery
- Margin expansion guided: **150–200 bps** in near-to-medium term

**Financial Projections (FY25–28E)**
- Revenue CAGR: **13.4%** | EBITDA CAGR: **13.6%** | PAT CAGR: **~15%**
- EPS: **₹12.5 (FY25) → ₹18.2 (FY28)**
- EBITDA margin: **17.2% (FY26E) → 19.8% (FY28E)**
- PAT margin: **12.7% (FY26E) → 14.8% (FY28E)**

**Return Ratios — Best in Class**
- ROE: **42.8% (FY26E) → 50.0% (FY28E)**
- RoIC: **75.6% → 89.8%**
- D/E: **~0.1x** — clean balance sheet

**Valuation**
- Trading at **56x/47x/41x** FY26E/FY27E/FY28E EPS
- EV/EBITDA: **41.2x/34.9x/30.4x**
- Premium to **Dabur & HUL** — justified by superior growth engines
- TP based on **~50x Sept'27E EPS** vs 5-year avg of **48.3x**

**Other Key Data**
- 1-year return: **+24.1%** | 52-week range: **₹593–₹814**
- Dividend yield: **1.4%**
- Promoter holding: **58.9%** | FII+DII: **36.4%**

**Key Takeaway**
- RM tailwind + digital growth = **double-digit earnings visibility**
- Margin recovery path is **clear & near-term**
- Digital portfolio scaling fast — **structural re-rating candidate**
- Clean balance sheet with **best-in-class return ratios**

**Home Decor Sector — Raw Material Shock | Nuvama Sector Update Mar-26**

**The Big Picture**
- **US–Iran conflict** disrupting Middle East energy & chemicals
- Near-term **margin pressure** across pipes, tiles & wood panels
- Broad-based **price hikes now inevitable**

**PVC & Plastic Pipes**
- PVC resin up **47% since Jan-26** → **₹101/kg**
- Crude up **43%** to **$86.7/bbl**
- Some manufacturers **stopped taking orders** due to price volatility
- **Q4FY26** expected to be strongest quarter on volumes & EBITDA/tonne
- Inventory losses of **9MFY26 reversing into gains**

**Jal Jeevan Mission — Residual Demand**
- JJM outlay: **₹8.7 Tn** | Rural tap coverage: **17% → 82%**
- High pending states: **Rajasthan (41%)**, **WB (43%)**, **Jharkhand (45%)**, **Kerala (45%)**, **MP (26%)**
- Residual JJM work offers **further demand upside** for pipe players

**Ceramics — Severe Stress**
- Gujarat Gas cut Morbi supply to **50%** from March 6
- **~100 Morbi units** already shut
- Up to **70% factories** could shut if propane stops
- **~1,500 export containers** stuck due to Hormuz tensions
- War-risk surcharge: **$4,000–5,000/container** vs normal **$300–600**
- Profit/container **~$300** — margins completely **wiped out**

**Wood Panels — Cost Inflation**
- Methanol **+46%** | Formaldehyde **+35%** | Melamine **+25%**
- Technical Urea **+24%** | Wax **+13%**
- MDF cost inflation: **7–10%** depending on grade
- Particle board RMs up **40–60%**
- Price hikes of **5–10%** already guided by smaller players

**Key Takeaway**
- **Pipes:** Near-term positive — volume & inventory gains in Q4FY26
- **Ceramics:** High risk — export margins destroyed, factory shutdowns likely
- **Wood Panels:** Price hikes inevitable — margin protection key monitorable
- Current PVC spike seen as **unsustainable** — correction likely ahead

**Why SIPs Thrive in Crashes: 2008 Proof**  

**Peak vs. Dip Reality**  
- **Jan 2008 Peak (NAV ₹16.84)**: ₹10K SIP → **79K** (XIRR 12.8%), **593 units**.  

**Power of Falling NAVs**  
- NAV crashes to **₹12, 9, 7, 6, 5** → SIP buys **~1,000 units** (more shares at bargain prices).  

**Dip Installments Win Big**  
- **2009 Bottom SIP**: ₹10K → **₹2.8L** (₹2.7L gain vs. ₹69K at peak).  
- **Similar holding periods**, but **exponential unit accumulation** drives superior returns.  

**Strategy Edge**  
- **Continue SIPs**: Rupee cost averaging maximizes long-term compounding.  
- **Legends top up**; **ultra-legends lump sum** on deep dips.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Falling markets = **SIP superpower**—buy more units cheap, ride the rebound for outsized gains like 2008’s 28x bottom returns. Stay invested.

**Time to Exit Axis Large Cap Fund?**  

**Consistent Underperformance**  
- Lags **Nifty 100 TRI** across all timeframes, hurting long-term compounding.  
- **Rolling returns (Mar 2016–2026)**: Fund at **~15.3% p.a.** vs. benchmark **15.5–16%**.  
- **5Y point-to-point**: **8.6%** (fund) vs. **11.2%** (Nifty 100 TRI).  

**Higher Downside Risk**  
- NAV drops **sharper than peers** during market corrections, amplifying drawdowns.  

**Portfolio Red Flags**  
- Heavy concentration: **Banks (25.6%)**, **Finance (6.7%)**, **Petroleum (6.1%)**, **Autos (5.9%)**, **IT (5.8%)**.  
- High **cash/derivatives exposure** caps upside; large bets on **HDFC, Kotak, ICICI**.  
- Recent weakness in **auto, oil/gas, consumer names**.  

**Better Alternatives**  
- **ICICI Prudential Large Cap**  
- **Nippon India Large Cap**  
- **Large Cap Index Funds** for SIP switches.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Axis Large Cap’s prolonged lag makes it a **clear switch candidate**—opt for outperforming peers to unlock superior compounding in your core large-cap allocation.

**HSBC Slams Brakes on Indian OMCs: Crude Squeeze Crushes Earnings**

**OMCs Face Marketing Losses**  
- Higher crude (~USD75/bbl) triggers sharp earnings cuts for IOCL, HPCL, BPCL due to mounting marketing losses.  
- Even moderate crude decks erase FY25's windfall margins (₹13-17/ltr), reverting to low-single-digit or negative profitability.  

**Downgrades Hit IOCL/HPCL/BPCL**  
- All three downgraded to **Hold** with slashed FY27-28E earnings (mid-teens to high-40% cuts vs prior).  
- Target multiples trimmed to/below 10-yr averages amid volatility and policy overhang.  
- Negative leverage: refining**HSBC Oil & Gas Alert: Crude Squeeze Hits OMCs Hard – Downgrades Galore**

**OMCs Face Marketing Losses**  
- Higher crude (~USD75/bbl+) triggers sharp earnings cuts for IOCL, HPCL, BPCL due to persistent marketing losses.  
- FY25's high margins (₹13-17/ltr) were outliers; pump price freezes now erode integrated profitability.  

**Downgrades to Hold**  
- HSBC downgrades IOCL, HPCL, BPCL to Hold: slashes FY27-28E earnings (mid-teens to high-40s% cuts).  
- Lowers target PE multiples to/below 10-yr averages amid volatility and policy overhang.  
- UBS echoes: trims targets (IOCL ₹175, BPCL ₹365, HPCL ₹340), warns $5 crude spike halves profits.  

**ONGC: Capped Upside**  
- Benefits from higher crude realizations but policy risks (govt caps ~USD75/bbl, regulated gas) limit supernormal gains.  
- Maintain Reduce: asymmetric downside from capex, project delays, subsidiary drags (HPCL/MRPL).  
- Target ~low-₹200s implies ~20% downside.  

**Positioning Shift**  
- Peak-cycle earnings over: build in lower, volatile marketing margins for OMCs.  
- Upside now valuation/technical, not fundamentals at elevated crude.  
- ONGC acts as policy-capped upstream utility, not pure crude play.  

**Key Takeaway**: De-rate OMCs, stay cautious on ONGC – crude spike flips sector from windfall to squeeze.

**Yatra Online – Business Snapshot**

**Business Model & Positioning**
• One of India’s leading **online travel agencies (OTA)** operating **Yatra.com**
• Offers **air tickets, hotels, holiday packages, buses, trains, cabs & activities**

• Strong **corporate travel positioning**
• Serves **700–800 large corporates** and **tens of thousands of SMEs**
• Among the **largest corporate travel service providers in India**

• Hotel network includes **~94k–105k domestic hotels/homestays across ~1,400–1,490 cities**
• Access to **2M+ global hotels**

• Expansion into **B2B segments**
• Added **freight forwarding (Yatra Freight)** and **MICE business** via acquisitions like **Globe Travel**

---

**Recent Operating Performance**
• **Q3FY26 revenue:** ~₹257–259 Cr (~US$28.7M), **~9–9.6% YoY growth**
• Growth driven by **better consumer mix, affiliate business & improved profitability**

• **Gross bookings:** ~₹2,176 Cr, **~21% YoY growth**

• **Air ticketing margin:** **↑ ~39% YoY**
• **Hotels & packages margin:** **↑ ~15% YoY**

• Profitability remains **volatile**
• One quarter saw **profit (~₹40 Cr) swing to loss (~₹129 Cr) YoY** at global entity level
• India-listed entity reported **~₹8.3 Cr PAT** vs **₹14.3 Cr QoQ**

• **TTM Revenue:** ~₹1,036 Cr
• **TTM PAT:** ~₹53–54 Cr
• **RoE:** ~2.9%

---

**Valuation Snapshot**
• **Market Cap:** ~₹1,500–1,600 Cr
• **P/E:** ~32–42x TTM
• **P/B:** ~2.2–2.8x
• **P/S:** ~1.7x

---

**Strategic Positives**
• Strong **B2B corporate travel moat**
• Added **145 new corporate clients in FY25** (~₹750 Cr billing potential)

• **EBITDA margins improving**
• **MICE and freight segments gaining traction**

• **Asset-light tech platform**
• Positioned to benefit from **long-term growth in Indian travel demand**

---

**Key Risks**
• Intense competition from **MakeMyTrip, EaseMyTrip and global OTAs**
• Valuation already **pricing strong growth expectations**

• Profitability sensitive to:
• **Airline pricing cycles**
• **Geopolitical disruptions**

**JEFFERIES ON BANKS: LIMITED BUYBACKS, DIVIDEND POTENTIAL AHEAD**

**Global Buyback Boom**  
- Global banks doubled buybacks to **>$260B in 2025**, driven by higher ROE, excess capital, and slow growth.

**Indian Banks' Parallel Setup**  
- Indian banks show **rising ROE** and **slowing growth** (still above global peers).  
- Jefferies sees **limited buyback scope** despite similar tailwinds.

**Dividend Cap Hike Unlocks Value**  
- Recent **hike in dividend payout cap** enables select banks to lift shareholder returns.

**High Capital, High Payout Candidates**  
- **HDFC Bank, Union Bank, Bank of India** lead with strong capital and payout flexibility.

**Capital-Rich but Cautious Players**  
- **Kotak Bank, CSB Bank** have high capitalization but have kept payouts low.

**Key Takeaway:**  
**Prioritize dividend upside in HDFC/PSUs over buybacks — ROE strength favors payout hikes in a capped repurchase environment.**

Jefferies On Banks

BROKERAGE RADAR

Evaluating Buyback Optionality Among Indian Banks

In 2025, buybacks by global banks doubled to >$260 billion aided by higher ROE, capital and slow growth

Indian banks' ROE has risen & growth has slowed, but is still above peers

So we see limited scope for buybacks, but with hike in cap on dividend, some can raise payout

HDFC, Union Bank, and Bank of India have higher capital and payout

Kotak Bank and CSB have higher capitalisation, but have kept payouts low
Iran 🇮🇷 has ~10× the population and far larger geography, yet Israel’s 🇮🇱 economy is bigger and its income per person is ~10× higher.

**India’s Credit Paradox – NBFCs & MSME Credit Gap**

• **NBFCs share:** ~21% of total credit  
• **Banks share:** Majority of remaining credit

• **Core issue:** Not a capital shortage, but a **credit translation problem**

• **Credit gap:** MSMEs, microfinance & small-ticket borrowers face **₹25–30 trillion deficit**

• **Economic role:** These segments contribute **~30–40% of India’s GDP**

• **Employment:** Employ **majority of India’s non-farm workforce**
**Strategic mapping of top transformer players in India**
